name: Sauteed Mixed Vegetables in Taro Nest
recipeInstructions:
95 g Thinly sliced lotus root
40 g Sliced water chestnut (approximately 1/3 cup)
40 g Soaked dried black Chinese mushrooms (approximately 5 pieces)
40 g Soaked black fungi (approximately 12 pieces)
40 g Snow peas (approximately 15 pieces)
40 g Celery
1 tbsp Chinese celery (optional)
40 g Carrot (a few slices)
Sauce:
4 tbsp Water
1 tbsp Cornstarch
1/2 tsp Salt
1/2 tsp Sugar
Garnish:
4 Soaked dried black Chinese mushrooms
3 Tomatoes
6 Baby corns (approximately 80 pieces)
6 Mustard green stems (approximately 115 g)
12 Soaked dried bamboo fungi (approximately 150 g)
Garnish Seasoning:
1 tbsp Oil
2 ts Salt
1 ts Sugar
4 cups Stock (or water)
(For taro nest, cut taro length ways into thin slices. Cut two-thirds of slices into thin strips for the edge of basket. Cut remaining one-third into strips 1.5 cm wide for the base of basket. Marinate with salt for 1 hour. Rinse under cold running water for 3 hours. Drain and mix with cornstarch. Form woven basket on inside of one mold. Lay second mold on top. Deep-fry mould-framed taro over low flame.)
Slice each mushroom into four. Halve each fungus. Remove snow pea strings. Cut celery diagonally. Cut carrot into patterned pieces.
Mix sauce ingredients well.
For garnish (optional), slice black mushrooms. Peel tomatoes, remove seeds and cut each into 4 slices. Cut baby corns and mustard green stems in half. Drain black fungi.
Bring garnish seasoning ingredients to the boil, add mustard green stems and black mushrooms for 1 minute. Add remaining garnish ingredients and bring to the boil again for another minute. Drain and trim.
Bring seasoning ingredients to the boil, add all sauteed vegetables except snow peas and cook for 1 minute. Remove vegetables.
Blanch snow peas in boiling oil for 1 minute, then remove when crisp. Pour excess oil out of wok.
Add sauce and all sauteed vegetables. Saute for 1 minute.
Arrange trimmed vegetable garnish strips around the basket. Place sauteed vegetables in basket.
